changed.from: Cross-fitted local-polynomial higher-order-influence-function R-score moments with separately budgeted clipped local Gram, numerator, and Holder-nuisance releases; matching lower-bound route via a causal localized fuzzy family jointly perturbing tau_P, pi_P, mu_{0,P}, and mu_{1,P}, followed by a central-(epsilon_n,delta_n)-DP tracing or fingerprinting contraction.
changed.to: Let q=alpha+beta and define the explicit private-HOIF envelope E_n(h,k)=h^gamma+(h/k^{1/d})^q+(n h^d)^(-1/2)+sqrt(k)/(n h^d)+k/(n epsilon_n h^d), over 0<h<=r_0 and 1<=k<=n h^d. The construction is a sample-split, Holder-projected nuisance pilot followed by a degree-floor(gamma) local-polynomial R-score and a k-dimensional localized second-order projection correction; clip the local Gram eigenvalues and scalar kernels, and add Laplace noise calibrated to sensitivities 1/(n h^d) for first-order moments and k/(n h^d) for the correction. Choose (h,k) minimizing E_n. The resulting derived candidate frontier is rho_n^{cand}=r_n^CATE vee (n epsilon_n)^(-gamma/(gamma+d)) vee (n epsilon_n)^(-(alpha+beta)/(alpha+beta+d)). Its proposed converse uses the Kennedy--Balakrishnan--Robins--Wasserman causal fuzzy family at outer scale h and microcell count k, combined with the DP output-contraction/tracing argument; the oracle branch is witnessed by the explicit causal two-point family in lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound.
changed.reason: Optimizing the estimator's bias, ordinary variance, degenerate-U variance, and global-sensitivity terms gives this closed candidate and exposes the possible additional alpha+beta privacy regime. The frozen prose handle names a route but does not provide the evaluable tuning objective or phase formula required to answer an OEQ.

A positive minimax lower bound and even the convention for the supremum in def:dp-minimax-risk require a nonempty law class; the frozen numerical constants do not themselves state compatibility with a probability density.

changed.to: L>e_0, so the propensity Holder ball contains an overlap-preserving interior constant and sufficiently small compactly supported alpha-smooth perturbations around it.
changed.reason: The frozen range L>0 permits the degenerate boundary L=e_0 (under the usual Holder norm including the sup norm), where overlap can force the propensity to a singleton and the cited causal nuisance lower family is absent.

note: maximality improve round: honest-scope rho_cand as conjecture + add sub-regime corollaries + two-point barrier + fix CCV loss-scale, and attempt private-HOIF upper + positive-density fuzzy converse (codex gpt-5.6-sol consult)
directive:
MAXIMALITY-ROUND DIRECTIVE (from D-orch, grounded in a gpt-5.6-sol maximality consult on the discharged writeup). The current writeup OVERCLAIMS: it presents rho_cand and epsilon_n* as the minimax frontier/elbow, but ONLY the two-sided bracket is proved. The alpha+beta "nuisance" regime is presently PRESUMED (an algebraic artifact of the term k/(n eps_n h^d)), not DERIVED. Fix the honesty AND attempt the field-clearing derivation, under these instructions.

== A. HONEST SCOPING (MANDATORY — do these regardless of whether derivations below close) ==
A1. The ONLY certified generic minimax theorem over the full positive-density class P_full is the bracket: c{n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n eps_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}} <= R_n^DP <= C{n^{-beta/(2beta+d)} vee (n eps_n)^{-beta/(beta+d)}}. Keep it as the headline generic result.
A2. RELABEL rho_cand as a CONJECTURAL optimized-envelope candidate and epsilon_n* as an ALGEBRAIC candidate boundary — NOT a proven minimax elbow. State explicitly that "KBRW causal fuzzy family + DP tracing" is a PROPOSED PROGRAM, not an established converse. Do not present the alpha+beta regime as derived.
A3. Do NOT claim r_n^CATE is the established full-class non-private benchmark: state precisely that the KBRW rate expression has a LOWER construction OUTSIDE the positive-floor class and an UPPER construction only under ADDITIONAL density/Gram stability (KBRW Remark 2 / App. B.8), so even non-privately upper=lower is matched by KBRW only on a restricted submodel, not the full positive-density class.

== B. ADD GENUINE CONTENT (HIGH CONFIDENCE — include these) ==
B1. Sharp sub-regime corollaries of the bracket (do NOT change the full theorem): (i) if beta=gamma then R_n^DP is order-equal to n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n eps_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)} over the full frozen class (bracket collapses to sharp); (ii) for every fixed C0, if 1<=n eps_n<=C0 then R_n^DP is order 1 (use the existing lower bump at a fixed small bandwidth + the clipped constant upper bound). State explicitly these do NOT resolve the beta<gamma frontier.
B2. Two-point BARRIER lemma (a real contribution): for two laws with the SAME positive design density p>=f_0 locally and the SAME overlapping propensity, conditional TV controls each arm-mean difference; with Delta=|tau_P(x0)-tau_Q(x0)|, gamma-Holder regularity makes |tau_P-tau_Q|>=Delta/2 on a cube of radius c Delta^{1/gamma}, so TV(P,Q)>=c Delta^{1+d/gamma}. Combined with the DP output contraction (lem:dp-output-tv-contraction), ANY common-design two-point/regression-coupling proof yields AT MOST the gamma privacy separation (n eps_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. Conclusion: the q=alpha+beta<gamma branch CANNOT arise from an ordinary two-point coupling — it requires a fuzzy-prior / higher-order-cancellation family. Remove two-point coupling from the proposed routes to the q-branch.
B3. Fix the CCV comparison: CCV reports SQUARED loss. In d=1 its private pointwise MSE term (n^2 eps^2)^{-nu/(nu+1)} has ROOT scale (n eps)^{-nu/(nu+1)}, matching this paper's gamma branch at d=1 under ABSOLUTE loss. Do NOT describe the absolute-error rate as an (n eps^2) rate; do NOT attribute the paper's arbitrary-d formula to CCV (CCV is d=1; the general-d scaling is the paper's own positive-design two-point + Laplace calculation).

== C. FIELD-CLEARING DERIVATION ATTEMPTS (make an honest, rigorous attempt; if either fails, RECORD the concrete obstruction and fall back to A/B — do NOT suppress terms, do NOT invoke "tracing" abstractly) ==
C1. Full-class private HOIF UPPER bound with COMPLETE sensitivity accounting: independent folds for nuisance pilots, local Gram estimation, first-order scoring, and the second-order U-statistic; a fixed compactly-supported spline/wavelet basis on the positive-density cube; clip propensity and Gram eigenvalues; define the final scalar mechanism explicitly. Analyze replace-one sensitivity SEPARATELY for records entering pilots, the Gram inverse, and each score fold — internal computation is NOT automatically private, so either prove global stability of the final map OR privatize the intermediate objects and include their errors. Prove a uniform bias-variance-privacy inequality INCLUDING the KBRW third-order random-design/Gram remainder. Optimize (h,k) ONLY after all terms are displayed. If the honest optimum differs from E_n, REPLACE rho_cand with the honest envelope rather than suppressing terms.
C2. Positive-density fuzzy CONVERSE for the alpha+beta branch (or a decisive refutation): fixed density bounded below on the ENTIRE outer cube + a smooth partition of unity over k microcells of width u=h/k^{1/d}; randomize PAIRED propensity and outcome-regression perturbations with amplitudes u^alpha and u^beta; impose overlap and bounded outcomes; tie h^gamma=4 u^{alpha+beta}; add explicit compensators on transition/filler regions so the CATE stays gamma-Holder while the design NEVER vanishes. Compute the one-record mixture likelihood and the n-sample mixture Hellinger or chi-square distance, then prove an approximate-DP output contraction at the required n eps_n u^{alpha+beta+d} scale with the n delta_n term controlled. If the positive filler leaves uncancelled first-order or h^{2gamma} terms, RECORD that as a REFUTATION of this candidate converse (report the exact residual), do not paper over it.

== D. FAITHFULNESS GUARDS (do NOT violate) ==
D1. Do NOT relax ass:local-density to zero/vanishing density anywhere; the KBRW zero-between-cells family is INADMISSIBLE under the frozen floor and may not be used as a lower family.
D2. Do NOT strengthen 0<beta<=gamma and do NOT add any crux-encoding assumption to force a sharp match.
D3. If a sharp two-sided theorem is obtainable ONLY on a named density-stable submodel P_stab (explicitly list the KBRW-B.8-type density-estimation + Gram-inverse remainder bounds ON TOP of the positive floor), state P_stab SEPARATELY and transparently; keep the certified bracket as the ONLY generic P_full theorem; require the submodel's LOWER family to ITSELF have positive density; do NOT silently import P_stab conditions into the full-class theorem.

== E. TARGET OUTCOME ==
Best: a sharp two-sided frontier if BOTH C1 and C2 close honestly under the frozen assumptions. Honest fallback (if C1/C2 do not close): the certified full-class bracket (A1) + sub-regime corollaries (B1) + the two-point barrier (B2) + rho_cand stated UNAMBIGUOUSLY as a CONJECTURE with the alpha+beta regime flagged conjectural (A2) + corrected CCV/KBRW scope (A3,B3), and optionally a transparently-scoped P_stab submodel theorem (D3). NEVER present the conjecture as proved. A derived honest bracket + barrier + conjecture is a valid maximal outcome; a false sharp elbow is not.

changed.from: Let q=alpha+beta and define the explicit private-HOIF envelope E_n(h,k)=h^gamma+(h/k^{1/d})^q+(n h^d)^(-1/2)+sqrt(k)/(n h^d)+k/(n epsilon_n h^d), over 0<h<=r_0 and 1<=k<=n h^d. The construction is a sample-split, Holder-projected nuisance pilot followed by a degree-floor(gamma) local-polynomial R-score and a k-dimensional localized second-order projection correction; clip the local Gram eigenvalues and scalar kernels, and add Laplace noise calibrated to sensitivities 1/(n h^d) for first-order moments and k/(n h^d) for the correction. Choose (h,k) minimizing E_n. The resulting derived candidate frontier is rho_n^{cand}=r_n^CATE vee (n epsilon_n)^(-gamma/(gamma+d)) vee (n epsilon_n)^(-(alpha+beta)/(alpha+beta+d)). Its proposed converse uses the Kennedy--Balakrishnan--Robins--Wasserman causal fuzzy family at outer scale h and microcell count k, combined with the DP output-contraction/tracing argument; the oracle branch is witnessed by the explicit causal two-point family in lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound.
changed.to: Let q=alpha+beta and define the formal algebraic objective E_n^alg(h,k)=h^gamma+(h/k^{1/d})^q+(n h^d)^(-1/2)+sqrt(k)/(n h^d)+k/(n epsilon_n h^d), over 0<h<=r_0 and 1<=k<=n h^d. Its exactly optimized value is rho_n^alg=r_n^CATE vee (n epsilon_n)^(-gamma/(gamma+d)) vee (n epsilon_n)^(-q/(q+d)); with s=min(gamma,q), this is n^(-kappa) vee (n epsilon_n)^(-s/(s+d)), and epsilon_n^alg=n^(-1+kappa(s+d)/s) is only its algebraic crossing. Neither E_n^alg nor rho_n^alg is a proved risk envelope, attainable rate, minimax frontier, or minimax elbow. The only certified full-class statement is lem:certified-private-cate-bracket, with sharp corollaries lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate and lem:bounded-effective-private-sample-risk. A private-HOIF upper program must separately privatize or globally stabilize nuisance-pilot, Gram, first-order-score, and second-order folds and retain the KBRW Gram/density third-order remainder. A q<gamma converse program must use a positive-density fuzzy family and prove a hypercube approximate-DP fingerprinting inequality; lem:common-design-two-point-barrier rules out an ordinary common-design two-point route. The KBRW nonprivate expression is not asserted as the established frontier of the full positive-density class: its published lower construction and upper attainability conditions concern different density scopes, with matching on a density/Gram-stable submodel.
changed.reason: The displayed optimization is correct algebra, but the old definition called it a private-HOIF envelope/frontier without complete mechanism-level privacy or a same-class converse. Complete fold accounting introduces a Gram/density third-order term G_n a_n(b_n+h^gamma) and private pilot/Gram errors absent from E_n, while the q branch still requires a new positive-density fuzzy DP tracing inequality. Relabeling the object as a formal conjectural objective removes the overclaim without changing its valid optimization.

note: round-3 cheap maximality fixes: remove unnecessary L>e_0 (repair oracle lower bound with pi=e_0, fixes soundness of the 2 sharp lemmas), generalize the two-point barrier to arbitrary P,Q, and tighten OEQ/TLDR/barrier prose (r_n^CATE reduction is open)
directive:
CHEAP HIGH-CONFIDENCE MAXIMALITY FIXES (from a gpt-5.6-sol confirmation consult). These are NOT the hard full-elbow research problem — they are soundness/faithfulness/prose corrections that MUST land before independent review. Apply all four; do NOT touch the (honestly-open) full-class frontier.

== FIX 1 (SOUNDNESS + faithfulness — remove an UNNECESSARY added assumption) ==
The solver-added assumption ass:holder-interiority (L>e_0) is UNNECESSARY and currently makes lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate and lem:bounded-effective-private-sample-risk NOT discharge over the truly-frozen class (they inherit L>e_0 via lem:certified-private-cate-bracket -> lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound). REMOVE ass:holder-interiority entirely. Repair lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound by taking the CONSTANT propensity pi_*=e_0 (it satisfies ass:overlap on the closed interval e_0<=pi<=1-e_0, is constant hence alpha-Holder, and is admissible whenever the class is nonempty by ass:model-nonempty) INSTEAD of pi_* in (e_0, min{L,1-e_0}). The lower-bound construction only perturbs mu_1 (not pi), so a constant pi_*=e_0 suffices. Re-verify that lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate and lem:bounded-effective-private-sample-risk now discharge over the full frozen positive-density class WITHOUT L>e_0. Do not introduce any replacement restriction.

== FIX 2 (STRENGTHEN the barrier — drop the common-design hypothesis) ==
Generalize lem:common-design-two-point-barrier to ARBITRARY P,Q in the frozen class (remove the "same covariate density and propensity on C_*" hypothesis). Proof device: for arm a in {0,1} write the arm-weighted sub-probability measure w_{a,P}(x)=p_P(x) pi_{a,P}(x) with pi_{1,P}=pi_P, pi_{0,P}=1-pi_P. By ass:local-density and ass:overlap, w_{a,P},w_{a,Q} >= e_0 f_0 on C_*. The finite-measure (L1) inequality ||w_{a,P} K^P_{a} - w_{a,Q} K^Q_{a}||_1 >= (e_0 f_0/2)|mu_{a,P}-mu_{a,Q}| pointwise, integrated over C_* and summed over arms, gives TV(P,Q) >= (e_0 f_0/2) integral_{C_*}|tau_P-tau_Q| >= c_TV Delta^{1+d/gamma} for ALL P,Q (via lem:holder-point-l1-interpolation), not just common-design pairs. Rename the lemma to reflect it is now a general two-point barrier. Keep the honest framing: it is a barrier for the two-point/TV-contraction PROOF METHOD, not an impossibility result for fuzzy mixtures.

== FIX 3 (PROSE scope-leakage — stop asserting the r_n^CATE reduction as established/required) ==
In the OEQ statement, the TL;DR, and Related work: do NOT state or imply that the privacy-free limit reduces to r_n^CATE as an established fact or a required property. That non-private full-positive-density-class reduction is ITSELF OPEN (KBRW match r_n^CATE only on a density/Gram-stable submodel, with a possibly-vanishing-density lower family). Phrase it as an OPEN DETERMINATION: "determine whether the privacy-free limit reduces to r_n^CATE over the full positive-density class (open; KBRW establish it only on a density/Gram-stable submodel)". Ensure the OEQ does not presuppose the reduction it is asking about.

== FIX 4 (PROSE — qualify the barrier summary) ==
Where the summary says the common-design lemma proves "any q<gamma branch requires genuinely fuzzy higher-order cancellation," WEAKEN to the exact scope: lem:common-design-two-point-barrier (now general) shows that any two-point argument certifying DP-output indistinguishability through the displayed criterion n{exp(eps_n)-1+delta_n}TV(P,Q)<=eta<1 cannot exceed the gamma privacy branch; it does NOT prove that every route to the q<gamma branch requires fuzzy cancellation. Minor: note the q=alpha+beta<gamma branch is strictly larger than the gamma branch only when n eps_n>1 (they coincide at n eps_n=1).

== FAITHFULNESS GUARDS (unchanged) ==
Keep ass:local-density positive floor; do NOT strengthen 0<beta<=gamma; do NOT add any new restricting assumption (FIX 1 REMOVES one and must not replace it). Leave the full-class sharp frontier honestly OPEN as oeq:causal-dp-frontier; do NOT attempt to force it. rho_n^alg stays explicitly algebraic-only.

== OUTCOME ==
After these four fixes the paper should be the maximal honest field-tier result: certified full-class two-sided bracket (no L>e_0), two genuinely-sharp sub-regimes (beta=gamma; n eps_n bounded) now correctly discharged over the frozen class, a GENERAL two-point barrier, honest CCV/KBRW positioning with the r_n^CATE reduction flagged open, and rho_n^alg as conjectural algebra — with the full-elbow frontier as the acknowledged research-level open question. Do not re-open the hard HOIF/fuzzy-converse problem.

Establish a two-sided rate and phase diagram that reduces to r_n^CATE when privacy is asymptotically nonbinding, and decide whether the pointwise-regression calibration r_n^regDP and private estimation of pi_P, mu_{0,P}, and mu_{1,P} generate one or more additional leading regimes. Prove any lower bound using a causal localized family rather than a regression stand-in.

directive:
SURGICAL FINAL FIX (from a gpt-5.6-sol verification consult: verdict = already_maximal_after_minimal_fix). Make ONLY the edits below. Do NOT re-derive, re-consolidate, restructure, or reword anything else. The math is correct; this is a consistency + presentation cleanup. Do NOT touch the honestly-open full-elbow frontier.

EDIT 1 (repair the causal lower bound to remove the unnecessary L>e_0):
In lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound, replace the sentence "By ass:holder-interiority and e_0<1/2, choose a constant pi_* in (e_0,min{L,1-e_0})." with: "Choose the constant propensity pi_*=e_0; it is admissible because ass:overlap is the closed interval e_0<=pi<=1-e_0, a constant propensity has zero Holder seminorm hence lies in H^alpha, and ass:model-nonempty guarantees e_0<=L under the sup-norm Holder-ball convention." Everything else in that proof stays identical (only mu_1 is perturbed; no propensity interiority is used anywhere).

EDIT 2 (delete the now-unused assumption):
Delete the assumption ass:holder-interiority entirely (its \begin{assumption}...\end{assumption} block). Do NOT delete ass:model-nonempty -- it is STILL used in lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound to obtain an admissible covariate density (the numerical density parameters alone do not guarantee one exists). Remove any remaining reference to ass:holder-interiority elsewhere.

EDIT 3 (restore the three results as NAMED lemmas/corollaries for citation + downstream Lean):
Restore, as named results with their original labels and statements, using the proofs already written inline in the oeq:causal-dp-frontier partial-resolution proof (move/copy them out; do not re-derive):
  - lem:certified-private-cate-bracket : c{n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n eps_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}} <= R_n^DP <= C{n^{-beta/(2beta+d)} vee (n eps_n)^{-beta/(beta+d)}}, proved by combining lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound (now with pi_*=e_0) and lem:private-local-polynomial-upper-bound.
  - lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate : if beta=gamma then over the full frozen class R_n^DP asymp n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n eps_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)} asymp r_n^regDP, with r_n^CATE=n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} and proved boundary eps_n asymp n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)}.
  - lem:bounded-effective-private-sample-risk : for fixed C_0>=1, 1<=n eps_n<=C_0 implies R_n^DP asymp 1.
The OEQ partial-resolution proof may then simply cite these named results. Ensure the stale references at/near line 154 (to these three labels) resolve to the restored named results.

EDIT 4 (consistency check only): confirm no remaining text references ass:holder-interiority or L>e_0, and that the OEQ proof's "pi=e_0 / no L>e_0" language now matches lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound.

directive:
D0.5-CLEARING REFRAME (from a gpt-5.6-sol D0.5-verdict consult; verdict = fixable_at_source). The D0.5 decision referee put oeq:causal-dp-frontier BELOW the field novelty floor: the advertised sharp frontier is open for beta<gamma, and the surviving sharp beta=gamma / bounded-n-eps regimes are pointwise-regression/constant-risk specializations. The math referee PASSED (all proofs correct). Do NOT weaken math and do NOT manufacture a false non-inheritance claim. Instead REORGANIZE the paper around its genuinely-causal field-level result: the general two-point barrier. This requires proto/statement/def changes, not an in-place edit.

== R1. PROMOTE the barrier to the PRIMARY THEOREM and organizing contribution ==
Elevate lem:general-two-point-barrier to a top-level THEOREM (new id e.g. thm:causal-dp-two-point-barrier) and make it the paper's HEADLINE result. Statement (already proved; keep the proof): for every pair of admissible observational causal laws P,Q in def:holder-cate-class, with Delta=|tau_P(x_0)-tau_Q(x_0)|, one has TV(P,Q) >= c_TV Delta^{1+d/gamma}; consequently ANY central-(eps_n,delta_n)-DP mechanism whose two-point indistinguishability is certified through n{exp(eps_n)-1+delta_n}TV(P,Q) <= eta < 1 can separate the two causal targets by at most C(n eps_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. Therefore the entire ordinary two-point / TV-contraction route CANNOT establish the conjectured nuisance-privacy branch (n eps_n)^{-(alpha+beta)/(alpha+beta+d)} when alpha+beta<gamma. Frame this in the abstract/TLDR/intro as the paper's causal-privacy HARDNESS contribution: a rigorous negative result delimiting how far the standard DP causal two-point method can go on the pointwise CATE problem, over arbitrary pairs in the frozen class.

== R2. DEMOTE the rate results to SECONDARY calibration supporting the barrier ==
Keep lem:certified-private-cate-bracket, lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate, lem:bounded-effective-private-sample-risk, lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound, lem:private-local-polynomial-upper-bound, lem:private-regression-calibration-algebra, lem:formal-algebraic-objective-optimization, lem:holder-point-l1-interpolation, lem:dp-output-tv-contraction as SECONDARY results (calibration / context for the barrier). The bracket + its beta=gamma and bounded-n-eps corollaries are supporting calibration, NOT the headline.

== R3. DISCLOSE the regression inheritance HONESTLY (addresses F2 the ONLY honest way) ==
Immediately after lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate, add an explicit remark/statement: at beta=gamma the sharp central-DP CATE rate is INHERITED from one-server private pointwise regression -- the upper bound is two armwise private regressions differenced, the causal lower family embeds a single regression problem, and overlap plus the two-arm structure change only constants, not the rate. Do NOT claim the beta=gamma rate is causally distinct (it is not, at the rate level). This honest disclosure is what F2 requires; a non-inheritance claim would be false.

== R4. REMOVE overclaims ==
Delete/soften: any headline or TLDR language about "determining"/"establishing"/"filling" the sharp frontier; the rate-optimal-privacy-budget and localization-rule "consumer" claims. State plainly that the full alpha+beta frontier (for beta<gamma) is OPEN, that any converse for the (n eps_n)^{-(alpha+beta)/(alpha+beta+d)} branch requires genuinely MULTI-HYPOTHESIS / fuzzy machinery (not a two-point argument, per the barrier), and that achievability of that branch is also open. Keep the honest CCV (squared-vs-absolute loss) and KBRW (vanishing-density submodel) positioning.

== R5. REFRAME the framing sections around the barrier ==
Rewrite the abstract/TLDR/project-justification/intro so the ORGANIZING result is the causal-privacy two-point barrier, the certified bracket + sharp identifiable regimes are the calibration that situates it, and the open frontier is the motivating backdrop. The OEQ oeq:causal-dp-frontier remains as the acknowledged open question that the barrier partially delimits (it rules out one route). Consumer/positioning: the barrier tells future work that reaching the nuisance-privacy branch needs fuzzy/tracing machinery -- a concrete methodological signpost, not a generic "future work".

== FAITHFULNESS GUARDS ==
Change NO exponent/constant/proof; the barrier and all lemmas stay exactly as proved. Do NOT strengthen assumptions. Do NOT claim beta=gamma is causally distinct. Do NOT claim the frontier is determined. This is a repositioning of already-proved content so the genuine causal field-contribution (the barrier) is the headline, with honest disclosure of what is inherited and what is open.

== OUTCOME ==
A theorem-led causal-privacy hardness paper: headline = the general two-point barrier (a field-level negative result over arbitrary causal pairs); supporting = the certified bracket and its sharp identifiable-regime corollaries with explicit regression-inheritance disclosure; honest open backdrop = the alpha+beta nuisance-privacy frontier requiring fuzzy/tracing machinery. Then re-run D0.5.
